retry_reason: Failure (NULL) means the resource was not found in the hash table; the caller may retry later if the resource is expected to be created (e.g., after releasing and re-acquiring the spinlock).
contract_summary: This is a lookup-and-get wrapper: it searches the lock resource hash table (under dlm->spinlock) for a resource matching the given name and length. If found, the resource's refcount is incremented via dlm_lockres_get and the pointer is returned to the caller. The caller holds the lock and must later release the reference with dlm_lockres_put when done with the resource. If the resource is not found, NULL is returned and no reference is acquired; the caller may retry after releasing the spinlock if a concurrent creation is possible.

judge.reason: Primary purpose is to look up a lock resource and acquire a reference on it via dlm_lockres_get, returning the referenced object to the caller. The hash table iteration and name comparison are necessary steps to locate the object, but the core semantic is a get operation.
judge.retry_reason: Failure (NULL) means the resource was not found in the hash table; the caller may retry later if the resource is expected to be created (e.g., after releasing and re-acquiring the spinlock).

check.reason: The function calls dlm_lockres_get(res) on the found resource and returns it. The access path res.refs.refcount and type chain dlm_lock_resource--kref--refcount_t are consistent with the callee information. The return value (index 0) is the critical variable.
